questions.text: What was the name of your first school and how old were you when you started there?
What did your school building, playground or classroom look like — any details you still picture clearly?
What was your favourite subject or class in primary/elementary school and why did you like it?
What was your favourite school lunch, snack or treat you looked forward to every day?
How did you usually get to school — walk, bus, bike, car — and what was that journey like?
What did recess or break time feel like — what games did you play most often?
Who was your favourite teacher from your early school years and what made them stand out?
Who were your closest school friends during primary/elementary years — what did you do together?
Did you take part in any clubs, sports, music, drama or after-school activities?
What was the funniest, most embarrassing or most memorable thing that happened at school?
Was there a school trip, assembly, performance or event you still remember fondly?
What was one moment at school when you felt really proud of something you achieved?
Did you ever feel like you didn't quite fit in or struggled to find your place at school?
Was there a teacher, classmate or school friend who influenced how you saw yourself?
What was one school challenge (academic, social, bullying, exams…) that felt big at the time?
Looking back, what skill or habit did you learn at school that you still use today?
Was there a time you felt unfairly treated by a teacher or the school system — how did it affect you?
What part of your school experience do you now realise shaped your confidence or self-view the most?
If you could go back to one school day and relive it differently, which day would it be and why?
What do you wish you had known or believed about yourself during your school years?
Is there one school memory that still makes you smile, cringe or feel emotional today?
What lesson from school (academic or social) turned out to be surprisingly important later in life?
Looking back, what one teacher or classmate do you wish you could thank or apologise to?
What part of your school experience do you most want your own children or future generations to have?
Is there a school experience you now see as a quiet turning point in your life path?
What do you hope people who knew you at school would remember about the person you were?
If your school-age self could see who you are today, what do you think they would feel?
